question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user use the complete ServiceEndpoint data (IP, domain, port) returned by toNodeAddress() to identify gossip nodes that share the same IP address (co-located nodes), and target that shared IP to simultaneously disrupt multiple gossip nodes with a single attack, preventing transaction gossip from reaching quorum?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNodes() returns node stake information (setStake()) alongside network addresses, allowing them to calculate the exact stake threshold needed to disrupt gossip consensus and target only the minimum set of high-stake nodes required to prevent transaction finalization?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cacheSize=50 limit in AddressBookProperties to cause cache eviction by requesting address book entries with varying parameters, ensuring that frequently-needed gossip node data is evicted from cache and must be re-fetched from the database on every request, increasing latency for gossip-dependent operations?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNodes() does not validate the gRPC metadata headers, sending requests with oversized or malformed metadata to cause header parsing overhead that degrades server performance and reduces the mirror node's capacity to handle gossip transaction queries?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() repeatedly to track changes in the address book over time (by comparing successive responses), detect when new gossip nodes are added or existing nodes change their endpoints, and time attacks against newly-added nodes before they are fully integrated into the gossip network?
